danger_signals.visual_form: Primary antagonist character model and associated visual theme
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat/boss encounter — world-threatening evil plot underway

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy character sprites (Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, Wizard, Grim Reaper, Mandarin, Juggernaut, Ultron, The Controller, Crossbones)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger — Red Skull's assembled army of high-threat adversaries

danger_signals.visual_form: Repetitive smaller enemy sprites in combat areas
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate tactical danger — standard combat threat in each level

danger_signals.visual_form: Shift to side-scrolling flight and projectile-heavy combat zones
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard shift — danger from airborne threats and projectiles

safety_signals.visual_form: Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor the Sub-Mariner appear as NPC helpers
safety_signals.meaning: Reinforcement/support — player is not alone in mission; allies provide tactical assistance

safety_signals.visual_form: Four playable Avengers available (Captain America, Iron Man, Hawkeye, Vision)
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and power — choice of combat approach based on character abilities

safety_signals.visual_form: Character-specific 'Avenger Attack' visual feedback (projectiles, energy beams, shield throws)
safety_signals.meaning: Escalation potential — player has high-impact defensive/offensive tools

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Avengers emblem (inferred from character selection and team branding)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: No friendly-fire or enemy variants noted; stable hero character identities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Safe zones with allied presence; allied NPCs appear in combat areas to assist

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Red Skull as visual leader/representative of the faction
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse supervillains (Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, Wizard, Grim Reaper, Mandarin, Juggernaut, Ultron, The Controller, Crossbones) — each with distinct visual identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Progressive level design where supervillains control different zones; final Red Skull confrontation is ultimate faction stronghold

world_state_to_visual.world_state: World under Red Skull's takeover threat
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Populated by Red Skull's supervillain army and generic henchmen across multiple level zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively attack players; escalating difficulty suggests spreading control/influence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each level features progressively more powerful supervillains (Klaw → Ultron → Red Skull), distinct visual designs mark territory control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters require character-specific strategy; villain-specific attack patterns indicate power levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Allied Avengers appear in combat zones (Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Allied NPCs provide active assistance in fights; suggests Avengers are mobilizing response

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shift between ground-based side-scrolling combat and flying/shooting sequences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay mechanics change (hand-to-hand brawling vs. ranged projectile focus); indicates environmental adaptation by enemies

environmental_storytelling.example: Generic henchmen → named supervillains → Red Skull boss; visual enemy advancement communicates growing threat scope

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character ability visibility as power fantasy
environmental_storytelling.example: Each Avenger has distinct attack types (Captain America's shield, Iron Man's beams, Hawkeye's arrows, Vision's energy) — visual differentiation makes each character feel unique and powerful

environmental_storytelling.technique: Ally presence as world state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Named Avengers and heroes appearing in levels suggests Marvel Universe mobilization and that player is part of coordinated response
Each supervillain (Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, etc.) has distinct visual design and combat approach — suggests Red Skull successfully recruited varied threats with different capabilities

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hand-to-hand fighting; flying kick during jump; shield throw as ranged special (Avenger Attack); uses flying machine for aerial sequences
Hand-to-hand fighting; 45-degree angle energy beam during jump; energy beam as ranged special (Avenger Attack); flies independently
Hand-to-hand fighting; flying kick during jump; arrow throw as ranged special (Avenger Attack); uses flying machine for aerial sequences
Hand-to-hand fighting; 45-degree angle energy beam during jump; energy beam as ranged special (Avenger Attack); flies independently
Appears as ultimate antagonist; boss encounter indicates end-game threat; distinct from henchmen/supervillain encounters
Each has distinct attack pattern and visual presentation; serve as zone controllers; require character-specific tactical approaches
Standard attack patterns; fodder for player progression; no unique behaviors noted

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Allied NPCs (Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appear periodically to assist in combat; indicate Avengers mobilization and player reinforcement

notes: T3 data tier: Information is empirical/qualitative from published sources. Sources do not provide detailed technical analysis of pixel-level visual design or color palettes; data reflects gameplay mechanics and narrative structure communicated through character types and encounter design. The game establishes faction conflict through clear hero/villain opposition (Avengers vs. Red Skull's army) with visual differentiation through distinct character sprites and supervillain encounters. World-state progression is communicated through level-based escalation and allied presence. No detailed information on HUD elements, minimap, or environmental color coding was available in sources.
